item.title: Learning Python after R
item.description: R vs Python? Some say both, This website and a lot of the content on it comes from my experience taking Computational Biology and Bioinformatics (SDS348) at UT Austin with Professor Woodward in Fall 2020. During the semester we focused a lot on using R to do data wrangling, visualization, and analysis, but we also covered a lot about Python. R is a programming language that’s used for data analysis for economists, academics, and people who love its unique set of packages that add onto its capabilities.

item.title: Project 1: Exploratory Data Analysis
item.description: Data Wrangling and Data Exploration ##Evan Samsky ems3938 Instructions A knitted R Markdown document (ideally HTML) and the raw R Markdown file (as .Rmd) should both be submitted to Canvas by 11:59pm on 10/16/2020. These two documents will be graded jointly, so they must be consistent (i.e., don’t change the R Markdown file without also updating the knitted document). The text of the document should provide a narrative structure around your code/output.

item.title: Project 2: Modeling, Testing, and Predicting
item.description: Modeling Instructions A knitted R Markdown document (preferably HTML) and the raw R Markdown file (as .Rmd) should both be submitted to Canvas by 11:59pm on the due date. These two documents will be graded jointly, so they must be consistent (i.e., don’t change the R Markdown file without also updating the knitted document). Knit an html copy too, for later! In the .Rmd file for Project 2, you can copy the first code-chunk into your project .

item.title: Projects
item.description: Written Reports The Food System of Bahrian: Analyzing Risks and Possibilities علاقات بين روسيا والشرق الأوسط Coding and Data Science Projects Wrangling & Exploratory Data Analysis Modeling, Inference, Prediction --
